problem_text: An economy reports the following spending for the year (in billions of dollars): household consumption C = 400, business investment = 60, government purchases G = 120, exports X = 100, and imports M = 120. Using the expenditure approach, what is this economy's GDP? Remember that only final goods and services are counted.

content.label: Final goods and services only
content.applies_when: only final goods and services produced this year are counted; transfer payments, used goods, and intermediate goods are excluded from GDP

content.label: Compute net exports (the trade balance) by subtracting imports from exports
content.action: subtract imports M from exports X to compute net exports NX = X - M
content.purpose: obtain net exports so it can be added as a single component in the GDP expenditure identity

content.label: Sum the four expenditure components -- consumption, investment, government purchases, and net exports -- to obtain GDP
content.action: add the four expenditure components, consumption C, investment INV, government purchases G, and net exports NX, to obtain GDP
content.purpose: produce the numerical GDP using the expenditure approach
